description: To 'noporu' is to work out what a thing should be: its form, relations, function, materials, or way of being made. A group may design only one part and revise it after the first trial. 'winora' plans an intended course, 'kisholu' records technical requirements, and 'pilewa' makes the thing; none turns a drawing into a finished wheel overnight.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and round into true-mid back 'o'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into stressed true-mid rounded back 'o'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip and finish with high rounded back 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'no' and 'po' gather the sound twice, first at the teeth and then at the lips. Final 'ru' gives the tongue room to move, as though a held shape has finally begun to turn in the hand.
usage_notes: The verb is transitive, and its event noun is a design. Intended function, participants, materials, constraints, evidence, and revision status can be stated around it. Exact drawings, dimensions, identifiers, and versions remain in their source record.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Design vocabulary lets speakers ask who a system is for, what it uses, and how it may be repaired.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Design can integrate function, material, structure, and living form rather than treating ornament as an afterthought.

semantic_domains.activity: specifying an intended artifact, system, or intervention
semantic_domains.aesthetic: shaping form in relation to material and use
semantic_domains.cognition: developing and selecting among possible relations
semantic_domains.creation: guiding making through an intentional specification
